_comment: TEMPLATE / SOURCE-OF-TRUTH for the COMPLIANT NOTIFICATION DISPATCH layer (Apify native integrations: Slack / Make / n8n / Zapier / generic webhook). Apply by configuring the corresponding Apify Integration on the report-builder actor/task, mirroring the message template + marking below. Nothing here is auto-deployed; <PLACEHOLDERS> are filled by YOU at deploy time. See docs/apify/notify.md. NO clickable Apify Console link is templated by design (Closure Mode).

voice._note: GOV.UK notification-banner / confirmation-page pattern: confirm 'the expected thing happened', use notifications sparingly, state what-next. Observatory/SecurityHeaders pattern: the GRADE LETTER is the headline, not a stream of per-event alarms.

_marking_note: Slack/Make/n8n/Zapier are OUTSIDE the platform. TLP:RED (raw url/storage-key/subject_label) is REFUSED to any external channel by notify-policy.js. The redacted rows that DO leave carry only content/markup HASHES, a change flag, coarse status and timestamps — proof that something changed WITHOUT a clickable destination. Redaction is delegated to integrations/exports/redaction-policy.js (never duplicated).

pacing.min_gap: weekly-or-slower (anti-compulsion floor; no high-frequency option exists)
pacing.distress_rule: a higher distress_risk_score SLOWS the minimum gap between notifications, never speeds it
pacing._note: A second notification inside the floor is SUPPRESSED, not sent. suppressed_until tells the operator when the next send becomes allowed. This is Closure Mode in the notification layer.

_scope_note: Notification pacing reuses the cadence gate, so dispatch is allowed only for scopes that are auto-schedulable. consented/brand/safety_evidence are not auto-notified (fail-closed).

slack.message_template_handlebars: Self-audit complete for {{subject_token}}. Exposure grade: {{grade}}. {{change_count}} change(s) since last check. {{what_next}}
slack._message_note: Handlebars over the NOTIFY-POLICY payload (NOT the raw Apify run document). Do NOT template {{resource.defaultDatasetId}} or any Console link — that would bait a click. Keep 2..4000 chars; mrkdwn-safe.

zapier._note: A zap can fan the redacted digest to email/Sheets/Trello. Because the payload is already TLP:GREEN-redacted and link-free, downstream zaps physically cannot forward a viewable artifact or a re-targetable URL.

make._note: Make scenario consumes the same normalized payload shape; map payload.title/text/what_next/grade/change_count to the destination module.

n8n.trigger: Apify Trigger node on ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED, then HTTP/Slack node consuming the redacted payload
n8n._note: Self-hostable n8n keeps even the redacted digest inside the operator's own infrastructure if desired.

generic_webhook._note: Lowest-common-denominator sink. The receiver MUST treat the payload as already-redacted and MUST NOT attempt to fetch the dataset behind it; doing so would re-introduce raw locators the marking deliberately stripped.

payload_contract._comment: The channel-agnostic shape notify-policy.js emits. A thin transport maps it onto each platform at deploy time.
payload_contract.title: string (reassurance-first headline)
payload_contract.text: string (plain, calm body; grade letter inline)
payload_contract.grade: string|null (Self-Exposure grade LETTER — the Observatory headline)
payload_contract.change_count: number (count only; never the items themselves unless redacted)
payload_contract.changes: array of redaction-policy projected rows (hashes/flags/timestamps; NO url/keys/labels below TLP:RED)
payload_contract.health: the output-health verdict that authorized this announcement
payload_contract.clickable_target: ALWAYS null (Closure Mode: no click bait)

never_dispatch_when: run output health is empty / malformed / failed / unknown (success != valid output)
scope is not auto-schedulable (consented / brand / safety_evidence)
inside the distress-aware anti-compulsion floor since the last notification
marking is TLP:RED (raw locators) to an external channel
channel or marking is unknown (fail closed)
